verdict_reasoning: KLK2 is a secreted trypsin-like serine protease produced by prostate epithelium, predominantly released into seminal fluid and serum. However, it is the target of active cell-surface-directed clinical programs (e.g., radioligand and CAR-T therapies against prostate cancer) based on documented binding/retention at the surface of prostate tumor cells, where a pool of secreted KLK2 remains associated with the plasma membrane / pericellular surface in a tissue-restricted manner. This is not classical TM topology; it is a prostate-lineage-restricted surface-accessible pool of an otherwise secreted protease. Not cell-state induced ecto-display, lysosomal exocytosis, PM cycling, or covalent TM tethering per se — best fit is tissue-restricted surface display on prostate (tumor) cells.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether prostate-tumor-cell surface KLK2 reflects wash-resistant anchoring vs. reversible pericellular equilibrium; could alternatively be secreted_only.
